profileSettings.title: Account Settings
profileSettings.description: Manage your profile settings, security, and more.

twoFactor.title: Two-factor authentication
twoFactor.description: Two-factor authentication adds an extra layer of security to your account by requiring a special code in addition to your password.

oauth.title: OAuth2 Clients
oauth.description: OAuth2 clients listed here inherit all permissions from their accounts.
oauth.noClients: There are no OAuth2 clients configured for this account.

twoFactorSetup.title: 2FA Setup
twoFactorSetup.qrCodeHint: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app.
twoFactorSetup.confirmHint: Enter the 6-digit code from your app to confirm.
twoFactorSetup.recoveryHint: Store these codes in a safe place. They will not be shown again!

user.description: Here's a list of your assigned servers.
user.noServers: You have not been assigned to any servers.

admin.description: Here's a quick overview of your server landscape.

networkTraffic.title: Network Traffic
networkTraffic.description: Inbound and outbound traffic for the last 30 minutes (KB/s).

servers.title: Server Management
servers.description: Add, edit, and manage all servers in your infrastructure.

addDialog.title: Add New Server
addDialog.description: Provide the details for the new server.

detail.description: Detailed metrics and status for this server.

aiSummary.title: AI Console Analysis
aiSummary.description: Copy and paste specific logs from the console into the text area below to get an AI-powered summary and troubleshooting tips.
aiSummary.loading: The AI is analyzing the logs...
aiSummary.ready: Ready to provide insights once you paste some logs.
aiSummary.fail: Failed to generate summary. Please check the console for details.

cpuMemoryChart.title: CPU & Memory Usage
cpuMemoryChart.description: Real-time CPU and Memory usage for the last 30 minutes.

networkChart.title: Network Traffic
networkChart.description: Inbound and outbound traffic for the last 30 minutes (KB/s).

fileManager.title: File Manager
fileManager.description: Browse and manage files on this server. Click on a file name to edit it.

editor.title: Editing: {{filename}}
editor.description: Make the necessary changes and click save.

settings.title: Configuration
settings.eulaDescription: Do you (or the server owner) agree to the Minecraft EULA?
settings.fileIdDescription: File Id for the modpack desired. This is found at the end of the URL for the specific file desired.
settings.ipDescription: What IP to bind the server to.
settings.memoryDescription: How much memory in MB to allocate to the Java Heap.
settings.portDescription: What port to bind the server to.
settings.projectIdDescription: Project Id for the modpack desired. This is found on the modpack's webpage on the right side.
settings.nameDescription: The friendly name of this server as it appears in the list.
settings.startOnBootDescription: Start the server when the node boots.
settings.restartOnCrashDescription: Restart the server when it crashes.
settings.restartOnStopDescription: Restart the server when it is stopped.
settings.enablePluginsDescription: Show or hide the plugin management tab for this server.

users.title: User Access
users.description: Users who have permission to manage this server.

addDialog.title: Add User to Server
addDialog.description: Grant a user access to this server.

users.revokeConfirm: Are you sure you want to revoke access for this user?
users.noUsers: No users have access to this server yet.

database.title: Databases
database.description: Manage the databases for this server.
database.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this database? All data will be permanently erased.
database.empty: No databases created for this server.

restore.confirm: Are you sure you want to restore this backup? All current files will be overwritten.

delete.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this backup?

empty.title: No backups
empty.description: Create your first backup to get started.

marketplace.title: Modrinth Marketplace
marketplace.description: Search and install plugins and mods directly from Modrinth.
marketplace.author: Author

installed.title: Installed Plugins
installed.description: Manage plugins already on your server.
installed.uninstallConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this plugin?

empty.title: No plugins found
empty.description: No plugins were found with that name.

notifications.installSuccess: Plugin {{name}} installed successfully. Restart the server to apply changes.

admin.title: Administration
admin.description: Perform critical administrative actions. These operations can affect server configuration and data.

installStatus.title: Installation Status
installStatus.description: Trigger the server installation or re-installation process.

delete.title: Delete Server
delete.description: This action is permanent. All server files and data will be erased.

deleteDialog.title: Are you absolutely sure?
deleteDialog.description: This action cannot be undone. This will permanently delete the server and all its data from the platform.

users.title: User Management
users.description: Create, view, and manage user accounts and their permissions.

addDialog.title: Add New User
addDialog.description: Fill in the details to create a new user account.

serversDialog.title: Servers for {{name}}
serversDialog.description: This user has access to the following servers.
serversDialog.noServers: This user is not assigned to any servers.

roles.title: Role Management
roles.description: View details and permissions for each user role.

addDialog.title: Add New Role
addDialog.description: Create a new role and assign permissions.
addDialog.descriptionPlaceholder: A short description of the role.

admin.description: Full access to all panel functions and settings.

user.description: Basic access to log in and manage their own account.

nodes.title: Node Management
nodes.description: Manage all deployment nodes for your servers.

addDialog.title: Create Node
addDialog.useDifferentHostLabel: Use a different host/port for server-to-server communication
addDialog.useDifferentHostDescription: This separate address is used when the main node needs to communicate with the new node. This is useful, for example, when the nodes are on the same network behind NAT.

deployDialog.title: Deploy Node: {{name}}
deployDialog.description: Follow these steps to set up and connect your new node.
deployDialog.step1: Step 1: Install AetherPanel on the new server.
deployDialog.step1Description: See the documentation for more details.
deployDialog.step2Description: If it was started during installation, run the following command:
deployDialog.step3Description: The file is located at /etc/AetherPanel/config.json. Replace its contents with the following:
deployDialog.step4: Step 4: Enable and restart the service.
deployDialog.step4Description: Run the following command to complete the setup:
deployDialog.successMessage: Your new node is configured and ready to use!

detail.successAlertTitle: This node is correctly configured and operational

serversOnNode.title: Servers on this Node
serversOnNode.empty: There are no servers on this node.

editNode.title: Edit Node
editNode.localNodeAlertDescription: The local node does not have any settings that can be edited. To change the displayed host, adjust the panel's master URL in the settings.

addDialog.title: Add New Database Host
addDialog.description: Follow the steps to connect a new database host.

step1.alertDescription1: Currently, only MySQL/MariaDB databases are supported for database hosts.
step1.alertDescription2: Is the panel and database not on the same server? Make sure to allow external access.
step1.userDescription: The username of an account that has sufficient permissions to create new users and databases on the system.
step1.passwordDescription: The password for the database user.

step2.userDescription: Use mysql -u root -p to access the mysql CLI.
step2.exitMysql: To exit the mysql CLI run exit.
step2.externalAccessDescription1: You will likely need to allow external access to this MySQL instance. Locate your my.cnf file (you can use find /etc -iname my.cnf).
step2.externalAccessDescription2: Open my.cnf, add the text below to the end of the file and save it:
step2.externalAccessDescription3: Restart MySQL/MariaDB to apply the changes. Make sure to allow the MySQL port (default 3306) in your firewall.

step3.hostNameDescription: A unique name to identify this host on the panel.
step3.connectionHostDescription: The IP address or domain name that should be used when trying to connect to this MySQL host from this Panel.
step3.portDescription: The port that MySQL is running on for this host.
step3.dbLimitDescription: Maximum number of databases. Leave blank or 0 for unlimited.
step3.linkedNodesDescription: This setting only sets this database host as the default.

templates.title: Template Management
templates.description: Select a template to create a new server.

common.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this item?

settings.title: Panel Settings
settings.description: Manage the global settings for your Aether Panel instance.

general.title: General Settings
general.baseUrlDescription: The link from which you will access the control panel following the format: http://localhost:8080
general.geminiApiKeyDescription: Enter your Google Gemini API key to enable artificial intelligence features.
general.hideAiAnalysisDescription: If enabled, the AI console analysis feature will be hidden for all servers.
general.allowRegistrationDescription: Self-registered users cannot perform any actions until permissions are granted. Disabling this only prevents direct registration, server invitations and the Users page are not affected.

discord.title: Discord Notifications
discord.description: Connect Discord webhooks to receive system alerts and notifications.
discord.alertsWebhookDescription: Discord webhook URL to receive server alerts (online/offline, resource usage, backups, etc.)
discord.reportsWebhookDescription: Discord webhook URL to receive periodic reports on the complete system status.
discord.statusWebhookDescription: Discord webhook URL to receive information about the status of the node/nodes.

mail.title: Mail Configuration
mail.description: Configure a provider to send transactional emails.
mail.providerDescription: Select the email provider you want to use to send emails from the panel.
mail.selectProviderPrompt: Select an email provider to see the configuration options.
